front: What is the Three-Schema Architecture in DBMS?
back: A framework that separates user views from physical storage structure using three levels: External (user view), Conceptual (logical/community view), and Internal (physical storage view).
The highest level describing user-specific or application-specific views of the database. Multiple external schemas can exist for different user groups.
The middle level representing the logical structure of the entire database, describing all entities, attributes, relationships, and constraints without physical storage details.
The lowest level describing how data is physically stored in the database, including storage structures, access paths, and indexing methods.
The ability to change schema at one level without affecting the schema at the next higher level. It ensures that modifications at one level don't require changes at other levels.
The ability to change the internal schema without affecting the conceptual schema. Changes like storage reorganization or indexing don't impact application programs.
The ability to change the conceptual schema without affecting the external schema. Changes to entity relationships or adding new attributes don't impact user views.
ANSI/SPARC (American National Standards Institute - Standards Planning and Requirements Committee) in 1975.

The process of transforming data between different schema levels. External/Conceptual mapping connects user views to logical structure; Conceptual/Internal mapping connects logical structure to physical storage.

front: What is the main purpose of Three-Schema Architecture?
back: To achieve data abstraction and data independence while providing multiple user views and isolating applications from physical storage details.

front: How many external schemas can exist in a DBMS?
back: Multiple external schemas can exist - one for each user group or application. All external schemas map to a single conceptual schema.

front: Which level of schema defines entity relationships?
back: Conceptual Schema - it describes the logical structure including all entities, attributes, relationships, and integrity constraints.

front: Which level of schema deals with record structures and access paths?
back: Internal Schema - it describes how data is physically stored including record layouts, indexing, and access methods.

front: What is data abstraction in DBMS?
back: The process of hiding implementation details while showing only essential features to users. Achieved through the three-schema architecture levels.

front: Why is Data Independence important in DBMS?
back: It allows system changes (hardware upgrades, storage changes, schema modifications) without rewriting application programs, reducing maintenance costs and increasing system flexibility.
